preview_rows.rr_repl_pattern_criteria_reported:
For the purposes of SCORE, to test H* and attempt to replicate the H* statistical evidence (if relevant), the two way interaction of the ANOVA model (tie strength x complaining on intended loyalty) must be significant at the p < .05 level. The pattern is: for participants with strong ties (vs weak ties) to the trainer, complaining (vs not) will lead to higher loyalty intentions. For the purposes of evaluating replication success for the SCORE T&E deliverable, this replication criteria is met if both (a) among participants in the strong ties condition, the group mean of the complaining group is numerically larger than the group mean of the not complaining group, and (b) among participants in the weak ties condition, the complaining and not complaining group means are within .1 units of each other.
For the purposes of SCORE, to test H* and attempt to replicate the H* statistical evidence (if relevant), the two way interaction of the ANOVA model (tie strength x complaining on intended loyalty) must be significant at the p < .05 level. The pattern is: for participants with strong ties (vs weak ties) to the trainer, complaining (vs not) will lead to higher loyalty intentions. For the purposes of evaluating replication success for the SCORE T&E deliverable, this replication criteria is met if both (a) among participants in the strong ties condition, the group mean of the complaining group is numerically larger than the group mean of the not complaining group, and (b) among participants in the weak ties condition, the complaining and not complaining group means are within .1 units of each other.

(1) mismatched signs, but have already verified that positive d from power analysis corresponds to equivalent effect reflected by negative d in report; (2) some weirdness with how stats are reported - t is positive in report but negative in SPSS output, and p-value is negative in report. the full text stats reflect values from the report, but the coded values have been reflected based on the SPSS output rather than the report document.

the pattern of means was directionally consistent with the pattern in the paper.
the pattern of means was directionally consistent with the pattern in the paper.
preview_rows.rr_repl_pattern_description_reported:
H* was not supported. However, the pattern of means was directionally consistent with the pattern in the paper. The results are as follows (M_Prevention: 6.7136, SD_Prevention: 1.73163; M_Correction: 6.7257, SD_Correction: 1.79048; t(919) = -.104, p = .917; Cohen’s d = -.007 CI:  -.136 to .122).
H* was not supported. However, the pattern of means was directionally consistent with the pattern in the paper. The results are as follows (M_Prevention: 6.6298, SD_Prevention: 1.79980; M_Correction: 6.7353, SD_Correction: 1.81150; t(2058) = 1.326, p = -.185; Cohen’s d = -.058 CI:  -.145 to .028).

preview_rows.rr_coder_repl_subjective_replicated_repo: Statistic and effect size text are taken from prose in report, but statistic and effect size values are taken from Analysis_updated.html in the Analysis component because they offer greater precision. The exception is the p-value, which is taken from the text because it is reported with greater precision there. Model params count is given based on 'Number of free parameters' = 91 from the output reported in the same file, plus 1.
preview_rows.rr_labteam_contributor: Erin Michelle Buchanan, Alexa Schlyfestone, Raquel Huggins
preview_rows.rr_repl_pattern_criteria_reported: Criteria for a successful replication attempt for the SCORE project is a statistically significant effect (alpha = .05, two tailed) in the same pattern as the original study on the focal hypothesis test (H*). For this study, a successful replication is a positive coefficient on the term for realistic threat at time 1 in the SEM predicting negative affect one week later.
preview_rows.rr_repl_pattern_description_reported: The SEM parameter estimates indicate that the path coefficient (b) for realistic threat at time 1 was b = 1.56, SE = 1.00, 95%CI [-0.41, 3.52], Z = 1.55, p = .1202667. While this effect is in the same direction as the original result, we would not consider this result to have replicated due to our criteria of p < .05.
